rice.name: Rice (Oryza sativa)
rice.nitrogen_deficiency_treatment: Apply 50 kg of Urea (46% Nitrogen) per acre to correct a moderate deficiency. Increase to 80 kg per acre for severe deficiency.
rice.moisture_deficiency_treatment: Trigger flood irrigation. Apply approximately 38,000 liters of water per acre to raise soil moisture by 10%.
rice.acidic_ph_treatment: Apply Agricultural Lime (calcium carbonate) at 500 kg per acre to neutralize acidic soil below pH 6.0.
rice.alkaline_ph_treatment: Apply Elemental Sulfur at 150 kg per acre or incorporate organic compost to lower alkaline soil above pH 7.2.

wheat.name: Wheat (Triticum aestivum)
wheat.nitrogen_deficiency_treatment: Apply 40 kg of Ammonium Nitrate per acre for moderate deficiency. Apply 70 kg per acre for severe depletion.
wheat.moisture_deficiency_treatment: Trigger sprinkler irrigation. Apply 19,000 liters of water per acre to raise soil moisture by 10%.
wheat.acidic_ph_treatment: Apply Dolomitic Limestone at 400 kg per acre for soil pH below 5.8.
wheat.alkaline_ph_treatment: Apply Gypsum (calcium sulfate) at 300 kg per acre or use ammonium-based fertilizers to counter pH above 7.8.

maize.name: Maize / Corn (Zea mays)
maize.nitrogen_deficiency_treatment: Apply 60 kg of Anhydrous Ammonia or 100 kg of Ammonium Sulfate per acre to satisfy high Nitrogen demands.
maize.moisture_deficiency_treatment: Trigger drip irrigation. Apply 25,000 liters of water per acre to raise soil moisture by 10% in critical root-zones.
maize.acidic_ph_treatment: Apply hydrated lime at 350 kg per acre to adjust pH upwards if below 5.5.
maize.alkaline_ph_treatment: Incorporate peat moss or sulfur-coated urea to safely reduce pH values exceeding 7.2.
